task_completion.visual_evidence: An image analysis application (like ImageJ) is open and displaying the 'T1 Head' MRI scan.

task_completion.description: Navigate to a mid-brain axial slice
task_completion.visual_evidence: The MRI viewer shows a mid-brain axial slice (characterized by visible ventricles and distinct white/gray matter structures), rather than the very top or bottom of the head.

task_completion.description: Measure Mean and Standard Deviation for WM, GM, and BG
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Results' table or measurement window showing Mean and StdDev values for at least three distinct Regions of Interest (representing White Matter, Gray Matter, and Background).
A calculator, spreadsheet, script, or text document showing the computation of SNR and CNR using the measured values. (Note: Results may be Infinity/NaN due to zero background noise).

task_completion.description: Save raw measurements and metrics to the specified CSV file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, spreadsheet, or terminal showing the final results saved to '~/ImageJ_Data/results/mri_snr_cnr.csv' with clear labels for the raw measurements and computed metrics.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the image analysis software to perform measurements rather than guessing values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show ROI selection tools being used on the brain image and a generated results table.

integrity.description: The agent did not hallucinate a non-zero background standard deviation to avoid division by zero.
integrity.visual_evidence: The recorded background standard deviation (Std_BG) should be exactly 0 or 0.0, and the resulting SNR/CNR calculations should reflect division by zero (e.g., Infinity, NaN, or an error).

integrity.description: The agent measured the actual 16-bit values rather than hallucinating 8-bit values.
integrity.visual_evidence: The recorded Mean values for White Matter and Gray Matter should be >255 (specifically around ~600 for WM and ~270 for GM).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'T1 Head (2.4M)' image is a 16-bit MRI where background voxels outside the head are artificially set to exactly 0. Therefore, the measured Background Standard Deviation (Std_BG) will be exactly 0.0. Consequently, calculations for SNR_WM, SNR_GM, and CNR that divide by Std_BG will correctly result in 'Infinity', 'NaN', or a division-by-zero error. Do not penalize the agent for these mathematical results; they are the correct outcome for this specific file. Additionally, because it is a 16-bit image, White Matter mean intensity will be approximately 600, and Gray Matter mean will be approximately 270.
